description: A network of interconnected strands; the pattern of relationships that link things together. 'Niro' names the mesh of connection—where pulling one strand moves all.
sound_symbolism: The word knits with structure. 'Ni' weaves with fine connection, threads crossing. 'Ro' grounds with networked stability, structure holding.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: plural with 'lo': 'lo niro' (webs). As verb: 'mia niro.' (I weave.) Related to 'phaliso' (network) but emphasizes the woven structure; compare 'selomi' (weave) and 'sima' (thread).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Web of interdependence—Indra's net where each jewel reflects all others.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Spider's teaching—the patient weaving that creates from within.
